referenced_section_hint: Prudential Standard APS 220 Credit Risk Management
description: This PPG should be read in conjunction with APS 220 Credit Risk Management.
This PPG should be read in conjunction with CPS 220 Risk Management.

referenced_section_hint: Prudential Practice Guide CPG 220 Risk Management
description: This PPG should be read in conjunction with CPG 220 Risk Management.
This PPG should be read in conjunction with CPS 510 Governance.
Consistent with CPS 220, where residential mortgage lending is material, the Board and senior management should specifically address it in the risk management framework.

referenced_section_hint: Paragraph 57 of CPS 510 — remuneration policy scope
description: CPS 510 requires the Board-approved remuneration policy to be aligned with prudent risk-taking and to apply to responsible persons, risk and financial control personnel and all other persons whose activities may affect the financial soundness of the institution.

referenced_section_hint: Prudential Practice Guide CPG 235 Managing Data Risk
description: CPG 235 provides guidance on managing data risk relevant to management information systems for residential mortgage lending.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment C of APS 220 — minimum interest rate buffer of at least 3.0 per cent
description: Under Attachment C of APS 220, ADIs must apply a buffer over a loan's interest rate of at least 3.0 per cent, unless determined otherwise by APRA.

referenced_section_hint: Prudential Standard APS 112 Capital Adequacy: Standardised Approach to Credit Risk — standard vs non-standard loan classification
description: APRA expects an ADI to have processes to determine whether loans approved through overrides would be treated as standard or non-standard loans for capital purposes under APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: Prudential Standard CPS 231 Outsourcing — ADI retains risk and responsibility for outsourced functions
description: As noted in CPS 231, an ADI retains both the risk and responsibility for material outsourced functions including origination and ongoing management of residential mortgage lending.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment A of APS 220 — security valuation practices
description: An ADI is required to have regard to the requirements governing security valuation practices as detailed in Attachment A of APS 220.

referenced_section_hint: Paragraph 76 of Attachment A of APS 113 — definition of loan in default
description: The definition of a residential mortgage loan in default for the purposes of triggering a full revaluation is referenced to paragraph 76 of Attachment A of APS 113.

referenced_section_hint: Prudential Standard APS 110 Capital Adequacy — enterprise-wide stress testing as part of ICAAP
description: APS 110 requires ADIs to conduct enterprise-wide stress testing as part of internal capital planning, including development of loss expectations on material credit exposures such as residential mortgage lending.

referenced_section_hint: Prudential Practice Guide CPG 110 Internal Capital Adequacy Assessment Process and Supervisory Review — stress testing guidance
description: CPG 110 provides guidance on approaches used for stress testing as part of the ICAAP.
